chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding the Disliked Nature of Traveling in the Month of Ramadan

text: 1972 - وَ رَوَى اَلْوَشَّاءُ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِي قَدْ جَاءَنِي خَبَرُهُ مِنَ اَلْأَعْوَصِ وَ ذَلِكَ فِي شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ أَتَلَقَّاهُ وَ أُفْطِرُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ» قُلْتُ أَتَلَقَّاهُ وَ أُفْطِرُ أَوْ أُقِيمُ وَ أَصُومُ قَالَ «تَلَقَّاهُ وَ أَفْطِرْ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1972 - Al-Washsha’ narrated from Hammad ibn Uthman, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A man from among my companions has sent me news that he is coming from Al-A‘was, and this is during the Month of Ramadan. Should I go to meet him and break my fast?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
I asked: "Should I go to meet him and break my fast, or should I stay and continue fasting?"
Imam (as) said: "Go to meet him and break your fast."
